title: Podman artifact pull leaves the previous local artifact around, without a name, breaking assumptions that artifacts are named
body:
### Issue Description

`podman artifact list` errors  on unnamed artifacts. Currently it's throwing ErrArtifactUnnamed when the user has pulled an artifact containing a (I believe) optional index descriptor `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name`.

Along a similar vein `podman artifact rm` only seems to support removing artifacts by name rather than digest: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/a5e55f5129a6073f1629ff42cea13f5c30a65927/vendor/go.podman.io/common/pkg/libartifact/store.go#L157



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Pull an artifact missing the `org.opencontainers.image.ref.name` index descriptor
2. Run `podman artifact list`
3. Observe that only an error is returned with no information about any artifacts.

### Describe the results you received

The ErrArtifactUnnamed error message.

### Describe the results you expected

A list of artifacts.

### podman info output

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

@rhatdan following up from matrix. Happy to open a PR for this if it's not working as intended.

comments.body: Hi @mehrdadbn9, I'd like to work on this issue. Please assign it to me.

i think this is working as designed and is not a bug.  that said, it was designed to a certain behavior and if users/we want to reconsider that, we certainly can.

@mehrdadbn9 i'm fine with you looking into this, but prior to providing any code, i would encourage you to somehow communicate what your intent is.

The comments around the `ErrArtifactUnnamed` code mentioned not being aligned on how to display unnamed artifacts so I think this is the intended behavior; apologies if this is mislabeled as a bug. The complicating factor is if ref.name is intended to be a mandatory annotation it's not currently being enforced at pull time allowing users to pull an artifact which then breaks listing (and removing) artifacts even for artifacts which supply the required name.

Just to offer a few thoughts though; one option would be for`podman artifact list` to follow a similar pattern to `podman image list` which renders `<none>` for both repository and tag in unnamed images. Barring that, perhaps rather than erroring list could return the list of named artifacts.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@baude This issue was marked stale and the committer working on it has closed their PR. Could I have this assigned to me? 

I can provide a deterministic reproduction of the issue. The error will arise anytime a mutable tag is updated to point to a new manifest.

Just as a warning: this will completely break `artifact ls`.

```
PORT=5880
TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)
ART1="127.0.0.1:${PORT}/test/failure:latest"
ART2="127.0.0.1:${PORT}/test/failure-new:latest"

printf 'version-one\n' > "$TMPDIR/v1.txt"
printf 'version-two\n' > "$TMPDIR/v2.txt"

podman run -d -p ${PORT}:5000 --name local_registry docker.io/library/registry:2 >/dev/null
until curl -fsS "http://127.0.0.1:${PORT}/v2/" >/dev/null; do sleep 1; done

# Publish v1 under ART1, then clear the producer copy.
podman artifact add "$ART1" "$TMPDIR/v1.txt" >/dev/null
podman artifact push --tls-verify=false "$ART1" >/dev/null
podman artifact rm "$ART1" >/dev/null

# First pull of ART1 into the local store.
podman artifact pull --tls-verify=false "$ART1" >/dev/null

# Publish a new manifest and move ART1:latest to it remotely.
podman artifact add "$ART2" "$TMPDIR/v2.txt" >/dev/null
podman artifact push --tls-verify=false "$ART2" >/dev/null
skopeo copy --src-tls-verify=false --dest-tls-verify=false \
  "docker://$ART2" "docker://$ART1" >/dev/null

# Second pull of the same mutable tag.
podman artifact pull --tls-verify=false "$ART1" >/dev/null

# This fails with: Error: artifact is unnamed
podman artifact ls

```

The root cause appears to be that pull writes to the same local name again, and the OCI layout code handles “same name reused for a new manifest” by removing org.opencontainers.image.ref.name from the previous descriptor instead of deleting that old descriptor. That leaves the old local artifact present but unnamed, and podman artifact ls then fails on that entry.

the addition of the `<none>` approach is a heavy one.  I'm indifferent if you want to assign it to yourself, but i do think this behaviour needs full agreement from maintainers.  Can you come to one of the community meetings and discuss?

It's been a few weeks since I was looking at this so it's not 100% fresh in my mind but if `<none>` is too heavy a lighter approach might be to simply not hard error on unnamed artifacts.

Regarding the community meetup though I'd be happy to join. It looks like the next opportunity would be this coming Tuesday the 7th? Is there anything I need to do to add an agenda item for this?
Just bumping this so I know whether to join tomorrow or not.

@ieaves Yes, please. Please add it to the agenda. https://hackmd.io/fc1zraYdS0-klJ2KJcfC7w Alternatively, if a full topic isn't needed, a question can be added under the `Open Forum/Questions` section.

Darn, it looks like I missed your message. I tried to join just now but it looks like the meeting has already ended. I can join next month but it may not be necessary if we can discuss the issue here. 

It's possible that I'm not appreciating the full contours as to why `<none>` would be such a heavy lift given the precedent of it's use for images. Is that something y'all could expand on?
